verses.text: Timothy, well reported, joins Paul, who circumcises him 'because of the Jews.' Wise accommodation. Paul yields in things indifferent for the gospel's sake.
Timothy, well reported, joins Paul, who circumcises him 'because of the Jews.' Wise accommodation. Paul yields in things indifferent for the gospel's sake.
Timothy, well reported, joins Paul, who circumcises him 'because of the Jews.' Wise accommodation. Paul yields in things indifferent for the gospel's sake.
They deliver the decrees; 'the churches... increased in number daily.' Strengthened. The settled question lets the church grow.
They deliver the decrees; 'the churches... increased in number daily.' Strengthened. The settled question lets the church grow.
Forbidden by the Spirit to preach in Asia, they pass by Mysia. Closed doors. God directs even by hindering.
Forbidden by the Spirit to preach in Asia, they pass by Mysia. Closed doors. God directs even by hindering.
Forbidden by the Spirit to preach in Asia, they pass by Mysia. Closed doors. God directs even by hindering.
A vision of a man of Macedonia, 'Come over... and help us'; they go, sure the Lord called. Open call. God turns them westward to new lands.
A vision of a man of Macedonia, 'Come over... and help us'; they go, sure the Lord called. Open call. God turns them westward to new lands.
They come to Philippi, the chief city, and abide there. New field. The gospel reaches Europe.
They come to Philippi, the chief city, and abide there. New field. The gospel reaches Europe.
By the river Lydia hears; 'the Lord opened her heart'; she is baptized. Grace opens. God Himself makes the heart receive the word.
By the river Lydia hears; 'the Lord opened her heart'; she is baptized. Grace opens. God Himself makes the heart receive the word.
By the river Lydia hears; 'the Lord opened her heart'; she is baptized. Grace opens. God Himself makes the heart receive the word.
A damsel with a spirit of divination follows them; Paul casts it out in Jesus' name. Power over evil. He frees her at the cost of trouble to himself.
A damsel with a spirit of divination follows them; Paul casts it out in Jesus' name. Power over evil. He frees her at the cost of trouble to himself.
A damsel with a spirit of divination follows them; Paul casts it out in Jesus' name. Power over evil. He frees her at the cost of trouble to himself.
Her masters, losing their gain, drag Paul and Silas to the rulers. Greed angered. Lost profit turns men against the gospel.
Her masters, losing their gain, drag Paul and Silas to the rulers. Greed angered. Lost profit turns men against the gospel.
Her masters, losing their gain, drag Paul and Silas to the rulers. Greed angered. Lost profit turns men against the gospel.
They are beaten and cast into prison, their feet in the stocks. Unjust suffering. The faithful are wrongly punished for doing good.
They are beaten and cast into prison, their feet in the stocks. Unjust suffering. The faithful are wrongly punished for doing good.
They are beaten and cast into prison, their feet in the stocks. Unjust suffering. The faithful are wrongly punished for doing good.
At midnight they pray and sing; 'a great earthquake' looses every band. Songs in prison. Joy rises even in chains, and God answers.
At midnight they pray and sing; 'a great earthquake' looses every band. Songs in prison. Joy rises even in chains, and God answers.
The keeper would kill himself; 'Do thyself no harm, for we are all here.' Mercy to the jailer. They stay to save the man who held them.
The keeper would kill himself; 'Do thyself no harm, for we are all here.' Mercy to the jailer. They stay to save the man who held them.
Trembling, 'What must I do to be saved?' 'Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved.' The way of life. Simple faith in Christ saves.
Trembling, 'What must I do to be saved?' 'Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved.' The way of life. Simple faith in Christ saves.
Trembling, 'What must I do to be saved?' 'Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved.' The way of life. Simple faith in Christ saves.
He washes their stripes, is baptized, and rejoices, 'believing in God.' Glad household. The whole house comes to faith and joy.
He washes their stripes, is baptized, and rejoices, 'believing in God.' Glad household. The whole house comes to faith and joy.
He washes their stripes, is baptized, and rejoices, 'believing in God.' Glad household. The whole house comes to faith and joy.
The magistrates would free them quietly; Paul, a Roman, demands open release. Just standing. He claims his rights for the gospel's honour.
The magistrates would free them quietly; Paul, a Roman, demands open release. Just standing. He claims his rights for the gospel's honour.
The magistrates would free them quietly; Paul, a Roman, demands open release. Just standing. He claims his rights for the gospel's honour.
The magistrates fear and beg them to leave; they comfort the brethren and go. Vindicated. They depart with honour, the church established.
The magistrates fear and beg them to leave; they comfort the brethren and go. Vindicated. They depart with honour, the church established.
The magistrates fear and beg them to leave; they comfort the brethren and go. Vindicated. They depart with honour, the church established.
